title: NBA insider points out two potential ‘stumbling blocks’ in Celtics’ pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "It would mean changing the entire structure of the way the Celtics play basketball.
" Giannis Antetokounmpo continues to be linked in trade rumors with the Celtics.
AP Photo/Aaron Gash Trade talks involving Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo are intensifying, according to ESPN’s Brian Windhorst.

title: Pioneering sports radio host Eddie Andelman dies
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Media The longtime host of "The Sports Huddle," Andelman was known for his quick wit and the millions he helped raise for cystic fibrosis research.
Eddie Andelman was a longtime sports radio host known for the live call-in show “The Sports Huddle.
” Frank O'Brien/Boston Globe Legendary broadcaster Eddie Andelman, who helped pioneer the modern-day sports talk radio format, has died at the age of 89.

title: Celtics have better odds than the Knicks for winning a title in 2027
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics The Celtics are the favorites to win the Eastern Conference and have the third-best odds to win it all.

Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff The Knicks clinched their first title since 1973 over the weekend, but when it comes to odds for next year’s NBA championship, New York already trails several teams including the Celtics.
According to both DraftKings Sportsbook and BetMGM, the Celtics have the third-best odds to win it all in 2027.

title: Harvard Book Store to open Boston location near Faneuil Hall
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local News The bookstore is slated for a fall opening.
Harvard Book Store plans to open a Boston location at 33 Union Street near Faneuil Hall and Quincy Market.

(Photo courtesy of Lily Rugo, Harvard Book Store) By Annie Jonas June 15, 2026 | 2:08 PM 1 minute to read Harvard Book Store plans to expand beyond its longtime Harvard Square home with the opening of a new bookstore in downtown Boston later this year.

title: Local obituary: Jeffrey Holland, 44, Boston fifth-grade teacher from Canton
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Jeffrey J.
Holland, a lifelong resident of Canton and devoted educator, passed away on June 12, 2026, in Boston, Massachusetts, at the age of 44.
Born on October 23, 1981, in Boston, Massachusetts, he was the son of Laura Holland of Canton and the late John Holland.

title: 20-year-old Mass. hiker dies after medical event in Monadnock State Park
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Joshua Luth, of Lancaster, died despite the efforts of first responders and good Samaritans.
Mount Monadnock, as seen from Tully Mountain in Massachusetts.
handout A Massachusetts man died after suffering a “medical incident” while hiking in Monadnock State Park in New Hampshire Saturday.

title: Accused Cape Cod restaurant shooter was acting in self-defense, lawyer says
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime “I didn't want to die,” alleged gunman Nicholos D.
Campbell, 26, told police after the shooting.

By Abby Patkin updated on June 15, 2026 | 1:23 PM 2 minutes to read A Cape Cod man accused of shooting and killing another patron at a restaurant in Dennis Saturday was acting in self-defense after he was stabbed in the head, according to his lawyer.
